results.question: How do I create a RingCentral Webinar using the API? What key settings are available, and how do I add registrants for a marketing webinar?
results.ground_truth: Create a webinar via the REST API specifying settings including: registrationEnabled (true for marketing webinars — gives each attendee a unique join URL), recordingEnabled, autoRecord, qnaEnabled, pollsEnabled, password, attendeeAuthentication. After creating the webinar, create a session. To add registrants for a marketing webinar, call the create registrant endpoint. Requires EditWebinars and ReadWebinars permissions. Set registrationEnabled=false for internal all-hands webinars (single shared join URL).

raw.answer:
Creating a RingCentral Webinar via the API is a **two-step process**: first create the webinar container (title, description, settings), then schedule a **session** (date/time) that hosts, panelists, and registrants attach to. ## Prerequisites Your app needs both permissions — without them, all Webinar API calls return `HTTP 403 Forbidden`: - **`EditWebinars`** — create/update webinars and sessions - **`ReadWebinars`** — read webinar data, registrants, attendees Authenticate with JWT (or another supported OAuth flow) before calling the API. ## Step 1: Create the webinar ```http POST /webinar/v1/accounts/{accountId}/webinars Content-Type: application/json { "title": "Product Launch Q3 2026", "description": "Quarterly product update webinar", "settings": { "recordingEnabled": true, "autoRecord": false, "registrationEnabled": true, "panelistWaitingRoom": true, "attendeeAuthentication": "Guest", "password": "s3cur3pass", "qnaEnabled": true, "pollsEnabled": true, "postWebinarRedirectUri": "https://acme.com/thank-you" } } ``` For a **marketing webinar**, set **`registrationEnabled: true`**. That gives each attendee a unique join URL for tracking and analytics. For internal all-hands meetings, set it to `false` — everyone shares one join link and skips formal registration. ## Step 2: Schedule a session After the webinar is created, schedule an occurrence: ```http POST /webinar/v1/accounts/{accountId}/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions ``` Required session fields include: | Field | Description | |---|---| | `scheduledStartTime` | ISO 8601 start time | | `scheduledDuration` | Duration in seconds (30–43,200) | | `timeZone` | IANA time zone (e.g. `America/New_York`) | | `localeCode` | Locale (e.g. `en-US`) | Optional: `title`, `description`, `panelJoinTimeOffset` (seconds before start for panel pre-sync). Save the returned **`sessionId`** — you need it for registrants and invitees. ## Key webinar settings These are the most commonly used settings (see the [API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/webinar) for the full list): | Setting | Purpose | |---|---| | `recordingEnabled` | Enables recording; must be `true` for other recording settings | | `autoRecord` | Auto-start recording when the webinar goes live | | `panelistWaitingRoom` | Place panelists in a waiting room on join | | `attendeeAuthentication` | `Guest`, `AuthenticatedUser`, or `AuthenticatedCoworker` | | `password` | Attendee access password | | `qnaEnabled` | Enable Q&A | | `pollsEnabled` | Enable polls | | **`registrationEnabled`** | **`true` for marketing webinars** (unique URLs per attendee) | | `postWebinarRedirectUri` | URL to redirect attendees after the webinar ends | Additional settings in the SDK include `panelistVideoEnabled`, `moderatedQnaEnabled`, `pstnEnabled`, `externalLivestreamEnabled`, and recording sharing/download options. ## Step 3: Add registrants (marketing webinars) Registrant APIs are intended for **external/marketing webinars** where attendees register in advance. Call the [create registrant](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Registrants/rcwRegCreateRegistrant) operation: ```http POST /webinar/v1/accounts/{accountId}/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions/{sessionId}/registrants Content-Type: application/json { "firstName": "Jane", "lastName": "Smith", "email": "jane@example.com", "externalId": "lid=123&oid=456", "source": "customer-invite", "joinUri": "https://acme.com/webinar/1234/join?id=5678", "cancellationUri": "https://acme.com/webinar/1234/cancel?id=5678" } ``` **Required:** `firstName`, `lastName`, `email`. **Useful optional fields:** | Field | Purpose | |---|---| | `externalId` | Link registrant to a CRM contact/lead/opportunity (supports encoded query-string values) | | `source` | Campaign attribution (e.g. `"customer-invite"`, `"search-ad"`) | | `joinUri` / `cancellationUri` | Custom join/cancel pages on your site instead of RingCentral-hosted pages | | `questionnaire` | Answers to custom registration questions | ### Custom registration flow A typical marketing integration: 1. Host a sign-up form on your site. 2. On submit, call the create-registrant API from your backend. 3. Send your own confirmation/reminder emails (optionally set `suppressEmails` via registration preferences). 4. Each registrant gets a unique `joinUri` for attendance tracking. ### Registration preferences (per session) Manage session-level registration settings with: ```http PUT /webinar/v1/accounts/{accountId}/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions/{sessionId}/registration ``` Notable preferences: `registrationStatus`, `autoCloseLimit`, `suppressEmails`, `preventMultipleDeviceJoins`. ### Tracking attendance - Each registrant gets a unique `id`. - When they join, they receive a `participantId`. - If both are present, they attended; if `participantId` is missing, they did not join. You can also subscribe to webhook events like `/webinar/registration/v1/company/sessions/registrants` for real-time registration and join updates. ## End-to-end flow ```mermaid flowchart LR A[Create webinar
registrationEnabled: true] --> B[Create session
date/time] B --> C[Add registrants
POST /registrants] C --> D[Send unique join URLs] D --> E[Track via participantId
or webhooks] ``` ## SDK note The Node.js/Python SDKs support Webinar APIs. Java and C# SDKs do **not** currently support Webinar APIs — use direct REST calls from those languages. For a guided walkthrough, see the [Webinar Quick Start](docs/webinar/quick-start.mdx) and [Creating webinars](docs/webinar/creation.mdx) docs in this repo.

mintlify.answer:
# Creating a RingCentral Webinar via the API

RingCentral Webinar creation is a **three-step REST workflow**: create the webinar container, schedule a session, then register attendees. The Webinar API is currently in **beta** and requires a RingCentral Webinar license.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

### App permissions
Create a REST API app in the [Developer Console](https://developers.ringcentral.com/login.html#/) with:

| Permission | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `EditWebinars` | Create and manage webinars/sessions |
| `ReadWebinars` | Retrieve webinar data |

Both are required. Without them, all Webinar API calls return **`HTTP 403 Forbidden`**.

Use **JWT auth flow** for server-side integrations.

### SDK packages and install commands

| Language | Install command |
|---|---|
| **Node.js** | `npm install @ringcentral/sdk dotenv` |
| **Python** | `pip3 install ringcentral` |
| **PHP** | `curl -sS https://getcomposer.org/installer \| php` then `php composer.phar require ringcentral/ringcentral-php` |
| **Ruby** | `gem install ringcentral-sdk` |

Java and C# SDKs do **not** currently support Webinar APIs — call the REST endpoints directly.

### Environment variables (`.env`)

```
RC_APP_CLIENT_ID=<your Client ID>
RC_APP_CLIENT_SECRET=<your Client Secret>
RC_USER_JWT=<your JWT credential>
RC_SERVER_URL=https://platform.ringcentral.com
```

---

## 2. Step-by-step API workflow

### Step 1 — Create the webinar

**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/webinar/configuration/v1/webinars`

**Required body fields:** `title`, `description`

**Marketing webinar example** (with key settings):

```json
{
  "title": "Q2 Product Launch Webinar",
  "description": "Customer-facing marketing webinar",
  "registrationEnabled": true,
  "recordingEnabled": true,
  "autoRecord": true,
  "qnaEnabled": true,
  "pollsEnabled": true,
  "password": "optional-attendee-password",
  "attendeeAuthentication": "Guest",
  "postWebinarRedirectUri": "https://acme.com/thank-you"
}
```

The response includes a **`webinarId`** — save it for the next step.

### Step 2 — Schedule a webinar session

A **webinar** is a settings container; a **session** is the dated, timed occurrence attendees join. Webinars are currently limited to **one session**.

**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions`

**Example body:**

```json
{
  "scheduledStartTime": "2023-05-10T09:00:00.000Z",
  "scheduledDuration": 7200,
  "timezone": "America/New_York"
}
```

- `scheduledDuration` is in **seconds** (7200 = 2 hours)
- `scheduledStartTime` uses **ISO 8601** format

The response includes a **`sessionId`** — needed for registrant calls.

### Step 3 (optional) — Invite hosts, cohosts, and panelists

**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions/{sessionId}/invitees`

```json
{
  "addedInvitees": [
    {
      "firstName": "Leia",
      "lastName": "Organa",
      "email": "leia.organa@newrepublic.gov",
      "role": "Cohost",
      "sendInvite": true
    },
    {
      "firstName": "Lando",
      "lastName": "Calrissian",
      "email": "lando@cloudcity.bespin.co",
      "role": "Panelist",
      "sendInvite": true
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 3. Key webinar settings

These are set at **webinar creation** (Step 1). Full list: [RingCentral Webinar API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/webinar).

| Setting | Description |
|---|---|
| `recordingEnabled` | Enables recording. If `false`, other recording settings cannot be set. |
| `autoRecord` | Starts recording automatically when the webinar goes live. |
| `panelistWaitingRoom` | Whether panelists are placed in a waiting room after joining. |
| `attendeeAuthentication` | Auth requirement for attendees. Values: `Guest`, `AuthenticatedUser`, `AuthenticatedCoworker` |
| `password` | Password attendees must enter to join. |
| `qnaEnabled` | Enables/disables Q&A. |
| `pollsEnabled` | Enables/disables polls. |
| `registrationEnabled` | **`true` for marketing webinars**, `false` for internal/all-hands meetings. |
| `postWebinarRedirectUri` | URL to redirect attendees when the webinar ends. |

### Marketing vs. internal webinars

| | Marketing webinar | Internal (all-hands) |
|---|---|---|
| `registrationEnabled` | **`true`** | `false` |
| Join URL | Unique per attendee | Single shared URL |
| Registrant API | **Use it** | Not needed |
| Tracking | Per-attendee analytics via `participantId` | Name-only prompt at join |

---

## 4. Adding registrants for a marketing webinar

Registrant API endpoints are intended for **marketing webinars** with external attendees. Set `registrationEnabled: true` when creating the webinar.

### Create a registrant

**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/webinar/registration/v1/sessions/{sessionId}/registrants`

**API Reference:** [rcwRegCreateRegistrant](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Registrants/rcwRegCreateRegistrant)

**Example body:**

```json
{
  "firstName": "Luke",
  "lastName": "Skywalker",
  "email": "luke.skywalker@jedi.org",
  "externalId": "lid=123&oid=4%4056",
  "source": "website-campaign-123",
  "joinUri": "https://acme.com/webinar/1234/join?id=5678",
  "cancellationUri": "https://acme.com/webinar/1234/cancel?id=5678"
}
```

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `firstName`, `lastName`, `email` | Core registrant identity |
| `externalId` | Correlate to CRM lead/contact/opportunity IDs |
| `source` | Track campaign origin (e.g. `"email-invite"`, `"search-ad"`) |
| `joinUri` | Custom join page URL (overrides RingCentral-hosted link) |
| `cancellationUri` | Custom cancellation page URL |
| `questionnaire` | Answers to registration questions (optional) |

**Requirements:**
- Caller must be the webinar **host** or an IT admin with **`WebinarSettings`** permission
- Registration must be **open** for the session — otherwise the call returns **`HTTP 403`**

Each registrant gets a unique **`id`** and a unique **`joinUri`** for tracking.

### Custom registration page pattern

For full UX control (common in marketing integrations):

1. Host your own sign-up form (e.g. `https://acme.com/webinar/1234/join`)
2. On form submit, call the **Create Registrant** API from your backend
3. Optionally set custom `joinUri` / `cancellationUri` on the registrant
4. Send your own confirmation/reminder emails (see `suppressEmails` below)

### Registration session preferences

Manage registration behavior via the [update registration session preferences](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/Registration-Management/rcwRegUpdateSession) operation (`rcwRegUpdateSession`):

| Preference | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `registrationStatus` | Turn registration on/off |
| `registrantCount` | Current registration count (read) |
| `autoCloseLimit` | Close registration after N registrants |
| `suppressEmails` | Suppress RingCentral registration emails (use your own) |
| `preventMultipleDeviceJoins` | Block joining from multiple devices |

---

## 5. Tracking attendance after the webinar

Each registrant gets:
- **`id`** — registrant ID
- **`participantId`** — set when they actually join

| Condition | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Has both `id` and `participantId` | Attended |
| Has `id` but no `participantId` | Registered but did not attend |

Use **`externalId`** and **`source`** to tie registrants back to CRM records and campaigns. Subscribe to registrant webhooks for real-time updates:

```
/webinar/registration/v1/company/sessions/registrants
/webinar/registration/v1/company/sessions/registrants?webinarId={webinarId}
```

---

## End-to-end flow summary

```
1. POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars
   → set registrationEnabled: true + other settings
   → get webinarId

2. POST /webinar/configuration/v1/webinars/{webinarId}/sessions
   → set scheduledStartTime, scheduledDuration, timezone
   → get sessionId

3. POST /webinar/registration/v1/sessions/{sessionId}/registrants
   → add each marketing attendee with firstName, lastName, email
   → optionally set externalId, source, joinUri, cancellationUri

4. (Optional) POST .../sessions/{sessionId}/invitees
   → invite cohosts/panelists
```

**Docs:**
- [Creating webinars](https://developers.ringcentral.com/guide/webinar/creation)
- [Registrants](https://developers.ringcentral.com/guide/webinar/registrants)
- [Quick Start](https://developers.ringcentral.com/guide/webinar/quick-start)
- [API Reference](https://developers.ringcentral.com/api-reference/webinar)

scores.raw_reasoning: Covers all ground-truth facts: two-step webinar+session flow, EditWebinars/ReadWebinars permissions, registrationEnabled for marketing vs internal webinars with unique join URLs, all key settings, and the create-registrant endpoint.
scores.mintlify_reasoning: Correct and complete on all key facts—permissions, settings, session creation, registrationEnabled distinction, unique join URLs, and create-registrant endpoint—with only minor API path/JSON structure differences in examples.

results.question: How long does a RingCentral OAuth access token last, and how do I get a new one without forcing the user to log in again? What happens when the refresh token also expires?
results.ground_truth: Access tokens last approximately 3600 seconds (1 hour). Use the refresh_token grant type (grant_type=refresh_token) to get a new access token silently before or after expiry — no user interaction needed. Refresh tokens for Auth Code flow last 7 days. If the refresh token expires, the user must re-authenticate from scratch (full OAuth flow). For JWT (server-to-server) flows there is no refresh token — simply request a new access token using the JWT grant at any time. Always cache and reuse tokens; calling the token endpoint too frequently triggers the Auth rate limit (5 requests/user/minute).

raw.answer:
## Access token lifetime

RingCentral OAuth **access tokens last about 1 hour** (~3600 seconds). The token response includes an `expires_in` field — store that with the issue timestamp and treat it as the source of truth for when to refresh.

| Token | Time to live |
|-------|--------------|
| Access token | **1 hour** |
| Refresh token | **7 days** |

## Getting a new access token without re-login

For **Authorization Code** (or **Auth Code + PKCE**) apps, use the **refresh token flow**. No user interaction is required.

1. **Enable refresh tokens** in your app’s Developer Console settings (“Issue refresh tokens”). They are **not issued by default**.

2. **Store both tokens** after the initial authorization, along with issue time and the `expires_in` / `refresh_token_expires_in` values from the response.

3. **Refresh proactively** before the access token expires (don’t wait for HTTP 401):

```http
POST /restapi/oauth/token HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Authorization: Basic {base64(client_id:client_secret)}

grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token={stored_refresh_token}
```

Each refresh returns a **new access token and usually a new refresh token**. Refresh tokens are **single-use** — always persist the new refresh token.

4. **Cache and reuse** access tokens for their full lifetime. The token endpoint is rate-limited to **5 requests per user per minute** (Auth group).

For **background/server apps**, run a scheduled job (at least daily) that refreshes tokens before they expire so users stay logged in indefinitely.

## When the refresh token expires

If the refresh token expires (after **7 days** without a successful refresh), **the user must re-authenticate from scratch** — full OAuth authorization flow with login UI. There is no silent recovery.

Other cases that also force re-auth:
- User revokes the app session
- Password change
- Admin “Absolute Session Timeout” policy
- App hits the limit of parallel active sessions (~5 per user)

## JWT flows (server-to-server)

**JWT auth has no refresh token.** Request a new access token anytime with the JWT grant (`grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer`). The JWT credential itself can be configured to never expire, so no user login is needed for token renewal.

---

**Practical pattern:** Auth Code apps → enable refresh tokens, refresh proactively before expiry, store the new refresh token on each refresh, and only send users through OAuth again when the refresh token is gone or invalidated. JWT apps → request a new access token with the JWT whenever needed.

mintlify.answer:
## Access token lifetime

RingCentral OAuth **access tokens last 1 hour** (~3600 seconds). The token response includes an `expires_in` field with the lifetime in seconds — store it with the token and issue time so you can refresh proactively instead of waiting for API failures.

| Token | Time to live |
|-------|--------------|
| Access token | **1 hour** |
| Refresh token | **7 days** |

## Getting a new access token without re-login

Use the **refresh token flow** (Authorization Code or Auth Code + PKCE). The user only logs in once; afterward your app exchanges refresh tokens server-side.

### Prerequisites

1. Enable **"Issue refresh tokens"** in your app settings in the Developer Console (disabled by default).
2. Store the `refresh_token`, `expires_in`, and `refresh_token_expires_in` from the initial token response.

### Refresh request

**Endpoint:** `POST https://platform.ringcentral.com/restapi/oauth/token`

**Headers:**

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `Content-Type` | `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` |
| `Authorization` | `Basic` + base64(`Client ID:Client Secret`) — required for server-side apps; **not** sent for Client-side web apps |

**POST parameters:**

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| `grant_type` | `refresh_token` |
| `refresh_token` | The stored refresh token |
| `client_id` | Required only for Client-side web apps |

**Example:**

```http
POST /restapi/oauth/token HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

refresh_token=BCMDFUMDRKV1MwMXx5d5dwzLFL4ec6U1A0XMsUv935527jghj48
   &grant_type=refresh_token
```

On success you get a new access token (another 1-hour lifetime), and usually a **new refresh token**. Replace both in storage.

### Best practices

- **Refresh proactively** before expiry using `expires_in` and `refresh_token_expires_in` — do not rely on `HTTP 401` from API calls.
- **Cache and reuse** access tokens for their full 1-hour lifetime; do not fetch a new token on every API call (token endpoint limit: **5 requests per user per minute**).
- Run a **background job at least once per day** to refresh stored tokens so sessions stay alive.
- **Refresh tokens are single-use** — each refresh invalidates the previous refresh token and issues a new one. Store the new refresh token immediately.
- For multithreaded apps, use a **dedicated token refresh service** to avoid race conditions.

### JWT alternative (no user login, no refresh token)

For server-side automation with a single service account, use the **JWT flow** instead. JWT flows do **not** issue refresh tokens — present the JWT again with `grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer` whenever you need a new access token, without user interaction.

## When the refresh token also expires

After **7 days**, the refresh token expires. There is no silent renewal — the user must **re-authenticate from scratch** via the full Authorization Code (or PKCE) flow.

What happens in practice:

1. A refresh request to `/restapi/oauth/token` fails (typically **`HTTP 400`**).
2. Per the OAuth token error guidance: do not retry blindly — **prompt the user for new credentials** and restart the authorization flow.
3. Your app loses access to that user’s account data until they log in again.

Refresh tokens can also be invalidated before the 7-day window if:

- The user revokes access (`POST /restapi/oauth/revoke`)
- The user changes their password
- An admin enforces **Absolute Session Timeout**
- The app hits the parallel session limit (usually **5** active sessions per user)

In those cases, expired or invalid tokens produce **`HTTP 401 Unauthorized`** on API calls (e.g. `OAU-128` — Access token expired), and you must obtain a new access token through re-authentication or a valid refresh token.

scores.raw_reasoning: Covers all key facts—3600s access tokens, refresh_token grant for silent renewal, 7-day refresh expiry requiring full re-auth, JWT flow without refresh tokens, and the 5 requests/user/min rate limit—with no significant errors.
scores.mintlify_reasoning: Complete and accurate on all required points including grant_type=refresh_token, token lifetimes, silent refresh, re-authentication when refresh expires, JWT server-to-server flow, and token caching with rate-limit guidance.
